As an Analyst on the Investment Data Operations team, you will play a critical role in managing and validating pricing and security master data for a diverse range of investment products. This role involves strategic oversight of data sourcing, validation, processing and distribution. The ideal candidate will possess an understanding of financial instruments and data management best practices and the ability to collaborate effectively with various stakeholders. ┬áYou will partner with Engineering & Technology, Portfolio Managers, and other internal teams to ensure accurate pricing data throughout the Investment ecosystem. This role requires availability to work from 11:00 AM to 7:00 PM ET, Monday through Friday.
We currently operate in a hybrid working model, whereby you will be required to work in-office 4 days per week.
Location(s): Charlotte, NC, or Milwaukee, WI

Perform daily valuation and validation of securities, including equities, fixed income, structured products, and derivatives.
Review and challenge pricing from external vendors, applying fair value methodologies in accordance with industry best practices and regulatory requirements.
Collaborate with internal teams to ensure data integrity and accuracy throughout the data ecosystem
Collaborate with technology teams to automate workflows and improve data quality controls

Job Summary
Aptive Resources is seeking a Senior Data Analyst to support artificial intelligence (AI), analytics, and data modernization initiatives within the Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) Office of Research and Development (ORD). This role supports enterprise AI activities focused on research portfolio analysis, operational reporting, AI-enabled analytics, and development of scalable data capabilities supporting research, policy, and operational decision-making.
The Senior Data Analyst will support data analysis, reporting, statistical programming, and analytics activities associated with strategic AI initiatives, research operations, and enterprise modernization efforts. The position will work closely with technical teams, project managers, operational leadership, and stakeholders to support data integration, reporting automation, AI pilot initiatives, and analytics-driven operational improvements.
The ideal candidate will have experience supporting analytics, reporting, statistical analysis, and enterprise data operations in healthcare, research, or enterprise operational environments.
This is a full-time (40 hours/week), 100% remote position.
It is currently funded through September 29, 2026 under the current contract period of performance, with potential for extension into the next option year based on contract continuation and program needs.
Primary Responsibilities
Support analytics and reporting activities associated with enterprise AI, research, and operational modernization initiatives.
Develop and maintain dashboards, reports, data visualizations, and operational analytics supporting leadership decision-making.
Perform data extraction, transformation, analysis, validation, and reporting activities across multiple enterprise data sources.
